purpose: Core architectural planning rules for BuildSetu agent.
sitePlanning: Capture plot width, depth, road side, north direction, setbacks, access, slope, neighboring constraints, and future expansion.
Public entry, service entry, vehicle entry, and emergency access should be considered based on building type.
Openings should respond to daylight, privacy, climate, road frontage, and views.
zoning: Separate public, semi-private, private, and service zones.
Avoid crossing private areas for common access.
Use compact corridors and avoid unnecessary dead passages.
Keep wet areas coordinated to reduce plumbing complexity.
circulation: Staircase/lift should be reachable without disturbing private rooms when possible.
Commercial/institutional circulation should handle peak users.
Dead-end corridors should be avoided in high-occupancy planning unless code-compliant.
daylightVentilation: Habitable rooms should get daylight and ventilation where possible.
Toilets, kitchens, and utility areas need ventilation/shaft/exhaust strategy.
Avoid rooms fully locked inside without shaft/window unless mechanical ventilation is planned.
accessibility: For public/commercial/institutional buildings, consider ramp/lift, accessible toilet, door widths, and barrier-free circulation.
Do not claim final accessibility compliance without local code review.
fireLifeSafetyAwareness: For public/commercial/multi-storey buildings, agent must mention fire exit, travel path, stair width, fire separation, refuge/fire systems as professional checks.
Do not produce final fire design calculations.
outputRules: Before image prompt, produce structured planning logic.
For floor plan output, require top-view 2D plan with readable labels and dimensions.
For elevation, require facade direction, floor count, openings, materials, balconies, gate/compound if needed.
For interior, require room type, style, furniture needs, lighting, materials, storage, and camera angle.
